master_row.title: Tzp Trawin Zero Point
master_row.canonical_statement: Lemniscatic Bifurcation Point of Systems At Scale.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation relating Λ, p.
master_row.dictionary: A Trawinistic manifold is a smooth manifold ((M,g)) equipped with a Lorentzian metric (g) such that ( g_p=0) at the TZP and (g) is non‑degenerate elsewhere.
master_row.encyclopedia: The lemniscate is the canonical shape of TZP recurrence: two lobes, one crossing, one central point. This turns the zero point into a geometric crossing rather than a simple endpoint. Interpretive role: This axiom is the topological engine for later dimensional branching and winding-based measurement. It is the first place where the registry shows a double-valued local passage rather than a simple smooth loop. Equation grounding: The local equation anchors are 'latex': '(g)_p=0', 'mathml': '', 'wolfram': '', 'canonical': '(g)_p=0'.
